lines.speaker: Jarvald
lines.text: What do you want from me outerlander? It is our policy not to associate with those not of our tribe.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where is your chieftain? I find it highly discriminatory to refuse to talk to someone on the grounds that they are not part of your tribe.

lines.speaker: Jarvald
lines.text: I don't rightly understand your speech outerlander, but my loyalty is with Chieftain Brundt.

lines.speaker: Jarvald
lines.text: He resides in our longhall; it is the large building over there, you should speak to him for he speaks for us all.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So what are you doing here?

lines.speaker: Jarvald
lines.text: This should not concern you, outerlander. I am awaiting other Fremenniks to join me on an expedition to Waterbirth Island.

lines.speaker: Jarvald
lines.text: It is a small crescent-shaped island just north-west of here, outerlander.

lines.speaker: Jarvald
lines.text: We have many legends about it, such as the tale of the broken sky, and the day of the green seas.

lines.speaker: Jarvald
lines.text: The reason I am travelling there is more serious Fremennik business, however. I doubt an outerlander would be interested.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can I come?

lines.speaker: Jarvald
lines.text: An outerlander join us on an honoured hunt???

lines.speaker: Jarvald
lines.text: Well.... I guess... I might be able to allow you to join us, although it is a breach of many of our customs...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh, pleeeeease? I really LOVE killing stuff!

lines.speaker: Jarvald
lines.text: Well... I remain unconvinced that it would be wise to allow an outerlander to join us in such dangerous battle, but your enthusiasm seems genuine enough...

lines.speaker: Jarvald
lines.text: I will allow you to escort us, but you must pay me a sum of money first.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What? That's outrageous, why charge me money? And, uh, how much does it cost me?

lines.speaker: Jarvald
lines.text: Ah, the outerlanders have stolen from my people for many years, in this way you can help my community with a small amount of money...

lines.speaker: Jarvald
lines.text: Let us say... 1,000 coins. Payable in advance, of course.

lines.speaker: Jarvald
lines.text: For this I will take you to Waterbirth Island on my boat, and will bring you back here when you have had your fill of the hunt. Assuming you are still alive to wish to leave, of course.

lines.speaker: Jarvald
lines.text: So do you have the 1,000 coins for my service, and are you ready to leave now?

lines.speaker: Jarvald
lines.text: I suggest you head to the cave with some urgency outerlander, the cold air out here might be too much for the likes of you…

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, actually I have some stuff to do here first.

lines.speaker: Jarvald
lines.text: As you wish. Come and see me when your bloodlust needs sating.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hey, I have to say, that's a fine looking hat you are wearing there.

lines.speaker: Jarvald
lines.text: It is actually a helm, outerlander, but the sentiment is appreciated nonetheless.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Wow, you Fremenniks sure know how to party. Well, see ya around.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Actually, I don't think I have anything to speak to you about…

lines.speaker: Jarvald
lines.text: Ah, you live yet, outerlander!

lines.speaker: Jarvald
lines.text: Have you had your fill of the hunt and wish to return, or are you still feeling the joy of the cull?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I wish to return to Rellekka.

lines.speaker: Jarvald
lines.text: Then let us away; There will be death to bring here another day!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I want to stay here.

lines.speaker: Jarvald
lines.text: Ha Ha Ha! A true huntsman at heart!

lines.speaker: Jarvald
lines.text: I myself have killed over a hundred of the dagger- mouths, and did not think it too many!

lines.speaker: Jarvald
lines.text: Greetings, [Fremennik name]!

lines.speaker: Jarvald
lines.text: Ah, and what a glorious name that is! Worthy of only the finest warriors!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Heh. Yup, you're right there.

lines.speaker: Jarvald
lines.text: So what brings you back to fair Rellekka? It has been too long since you have drunk in the longhall with us and sang of your battles!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So what are you doing here?

lines.speaker: Jarvald
lines.text: You have not heard, [Fremennik name]? I am leading an expedition to Waterbirth Island!

lines.speaker: Jarvald
lines.text: You have not ever travelled to Waterbirth Island, [Fremennik name]? I am surprised, it is a place of outstanding natural beauty.

lines.speaker: Jarvald
lines.text: Or at least it used to be! But things have now changed...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Changed? How do you mean, changed?

lines.speaker: Jarvald
lines.text: It seems as though the sea-beasts known to us as the dagger-mouths have begun their hatching once again... And there may be others of their ilk there too.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Dagger-mouths?

lines.speaker: Jarvald
lines.text: Aye, the dagger-mouths! The vile creatures lived near here once, but we had thought them all driven back to the ocean depths many moons past.

lines.speaker: Jarvald
lines.text: I can only imagine a dagger-mouth queen has nested somewhere nearby, and spawned her foul brood under the sea once more, and some of them have migrated to fair Waterbirth Island.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So you're scared they might attack Rellekka?

lines.speaker: Jarvald
lines.text: Scared? Ha ha ha!

lines.speaker: Jarvald
lines.text: You wound us with your questioning, [Fremennik name]!

lines.speaker: Jarvald
lines.text: We are glad the dagger-mouths have returned to these shores, for it means we will get the chance to hunt them once again as our ancestors did!

lines.speaker: Jarvald
lines.text: When treated in the correct manner, the creatures' remains can be used to make fine battleworthy armour!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can I come?

lines.speaker: Jarvald
lines.text: Of course, [Fremennik name]! Your presence is more than welcome on this cull! You wish to leave now?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, actually I have some stuff to do here first.

lines.speaker: Jarvald
lines.text: As you wish. Come and see me when your bloodlust needs sating.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hey, I have to say, that's a fine looking hat you are wearing there.

lines.speaker: Jarvald
lines.text: Aye, that it is [Fremennik name]! Skulgrimen fashioned it for me from the carcass of one of the monsters on Waterbirth Island after our last hunt!

lines.speaker: Jarvald
lines.text: I hope to kill enough creatures to fashion some fine armour as well when next we leave!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Wow, you Fremenniks sure know how to party. Well, see ya around.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I picked up this, I was wondering if you could tell me about it...

lines.speaker: Jarvald
lines.text: Ah yes... The dagger-mouths' skin makes a good basis for many types of armour!

lines.speaker: Jarvald
lines.text: Skulgrimen has made many sturdy helms from shells like that!

lines.speaker: Jarvald
lines.text: Skulgrimen once made a fine set of body armour from that kind of shell!

lines.speaker: Jarvald
lines.text: Aha! That could be made into some fine leg armour by Skulgrimen, I'll wager!

lines.speaker: Jarvald
lines.text: Hmmmm... I believe Peer could make a fine seers helm with that...

lines.speaker: Jarvald
lines.text: Many moons past, Peer had some fine bodyarmour made from just such bones.

lines.speaker: Jarvald
lines.text: Peer could use that to make some mighty robes, I'll wager.

lines.speaker: Jarvald
lines.text: Sigli might be able to make you some sturdy headgear with that!

lines.speaker: Jarvald
lines.text: That could make a fine piece of hunting armour, if I know Sigli like I think I do.

lines.speaker: Jarvald
lines.text: I'll wager Sigli can make that into some hunting legs for you.

lines.speaker: Jarvald
lines.text: Ah, it is always a pleasure [Fremennik name]! Now did you want something more from me?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Actually, I don't think I have anything to speak to you about…

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Actually, I don't think I have anything to speak to you about...

lines.speaker: Jarvald
lines.text: Ah, [Fremennik name]! Such glorious battle makes you feel glad of life, does it not?

lines.speaker: Jarvald
lines.text: So what say you, stay here for the hunt, or return home to sweet Rellekka to feast and drink with your tribe?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I wish to return to Rellekka.

lines.speaker: Jarvald
lines.text: Then let us away; There will be death to bring here another day!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I want to stay here.

lines.speaker: Jarvald
lines.text: Ha Ha Ha! A true huntsman at heart!

lines.speaker: Jarvald
lines.text: I myself have killed over a hundred of the dagger- mouths, and did not think it too many!

sections.title: When using something other than a Fremennik armour component on Jarvald

lines.speaker: Jarvald
lines.text: Thank you, but I have no need for that!

sections.title: While Exiled During The Fremennik Exiles

lines.speaker: Jarvald
lines.text: Forgive me exile, though I can't allow you to board my ship.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Please Jarvald, it's really important!

lines.speaker: Jarvald
lines.text: Very well... for old times sake.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Fair enough.

lines.speaker: Jarvald
lines.text: I shouldn't do this... but for old times sake.

lines.speaker: Jarvald
lines.text: Do you wish to return to Rellekka?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes please.

lines.speaker: Jarvald
lines.text: Very well, but do make sure you're not spotted leaving my ship.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No thanks.

lines.speaker: Jarvald
lines.text: Quickly [Fremennik name]. Once you arrive, leave my ship and make sure you're not spotted!
